# sonatyperepo_security_ssl_truststore (Resource)

Manage SSL certificates in the Nexus truststore.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "sonatyperepo_security_ssl_truststore" "my_ca" {
  pem = file("${path.module}/certs/MY_CA.pem")
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `pem` (String) PEM-encoded certificate to add to the truststore

### Read-Only

- `expires_on` (Number) Certificate expires on (epoch milliseconds)
- `fingerprint` (String) SHA-1 fingerprint of the certificate
- `id` (String) Certificate ID
- `issued_on` (Number) Certificate issued on (epoch milliseconds)
- `issuer_common_name` (String) Issuer common name
- `issuer_organization` (String) Issuer organization
- `issuer_organizational_unit` (String) Issuer organizational unit
- `last_updated` (String) String representation of the date/time the resource was last changed
- `serial_number` (String) Serial number of the certificate
- `subject_common_name` (String) Subject common name
- `subject_organization` (String) Subject organization
- `subject_organizational_unit` (String) Subject organizational unit

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# Import an existing SSL certificate from the truststore by its ID.
terraform import sonatyperepo_security_ssl_truststore.my_ca <certificate-id>
```
